story.title: Multi-camera workspace: per-camera images, polygons, and YAML behind a camera tab strip
story.i_want: to switch between my cameras inside the editor, each keeping its own frame, polygons, and YAML, with a merged full-config export
story.so_that: I can configure my whole installation in one session instead of one browser tab per camera

acceptance_criteria: Given cameras exist (added in Camera Builder or imported), when the Editor tab is active, then a horizontal camera tab strip renders above the canvas with one tab per camera plus a '+ Camera' tab that jumps to the Camera Builder

Given the user switches camera tabs, when the switch completes, then the canvas shows that camera's image (or its re-upload prompt), the item list shows only that camera's polygons, and the YAML panel shows that camera's snippet — with zero leakage between cameras

Given polygons exist on multiple cameras, when the YAML panel's scope toggle is set to 'All cameras', then a merged cameras: block emits each camera's ffmpeg/detect settings with its zones/motion/objects nested under the camera key, ready to paste as a full config

Given v0.1-era or pre-spec-08 persisted data (polygons under the 'default' pseudo-camera), when the app loads, then those polygons appear under a 'default' tab and can be reassigned by an 'Assign to camera…' action on the item list

Given a camera is deleted in Camera Builder, when deletion fires, then its polygons and image meta are removed with it, covered by the same undo toast (undo restores camera, polygons, and meta together)

Given a multi-camera YAML import (spec 06), when imported, then cameras found in the config are created as workspace cameras and their polygons land on their own tabs, replacing the single-camera picker flow
constraints: State already shaped for this (polygonsByCamera, imagesMeta keyed by cameraId from spec 01) — this spec must not introduce a second polygon store
Per-camera images held in memory as dataURLs in a non-persisted runtime map (Images[cameraId]); localStorage continues to store meta only
Camera tab strip is the only new layout element; the 3-column grid and full-viewport lock are preserved at 1440p
YAML scope toggle: 'This camera' (default — snippet with zones/motion/objects only, suitable for pasting into an existing camera block) and 'All cameras' (full cameras: block)
Switching tabs preserves any in-progress draft on the camera it was drawn on (draft is per-camera state)
schemaVersion bumps to 3; Persist migration from v2 maps 'default' polygons into the default pseudo-camera tab
edge_cases: Zero cameras defined — editor behaves exactly as today under an implicit 'default' tab; tab strip hidden until a second context exists
Camera renamed in Camera Builder — polygonsByCamera key is the stable camera id, so rename only changes labels and YAML keys; zone-name-vs-camera-name validation re-runs against the new name
Two cameras with the same name — blocked by Camera Builder validation (names must be unique, inline error)
Import creates a camera that already exists by name — polygons merge into the existing camera with rename-on-collision per spec 06 semantics
20+ cameras — tab strip scrolls horizontally; no dropdown needed in v0.2
'default' tab with zero polygons after reassignment — tab disappears
risks: Medium: largest UI change of v0.2; viewport-lock regressions at 1440p and the 900px mobile stack need explicit QA
Medium: undo of camera deletion must atomically restore three state slices (camera, polygons, image meta) — snapshot the whole workspace slice for this toast
Low: memory growth from N in-memory dataURLs — a dozen 1080p frames is tens of MB, acceptable for a desktop tool

open_questions.question: Does the per-camera YAML snippet include the camera's ffmpeg/detect block or only polygons?

options.label: Polygons only in 'This camera' scope (Recommended)
options.info: Matches the dominant use case: pasting zones into an existing config. Full settings available via 'All cameras' scope.

options.label: Always include full camera block
options.info: Self-contained but forces users to strip ffmpeg settings they already have.

tasks.description: Add camera tab strip component above canvas (tabs from Store.cameras + default pseudo-tab + '+ Camera'); implement Store.setActiveCamera with per-camera draft preservation
Move runtime image handling to Images map keyed by cameraId; per-camera re-upload prompts from imagesMeta; wire upload/paste to active camera
Implement YAML scope toggle and merged all-cameras emission (ffmpeg/detect + nested zones/motion/objects per camera); retire the separate Camera Builder YAML panel into the merged view
Persist schema v3 migration (default-bucket mapping), atomic camera-delete undo, 'Assign to camera…' action, unique-camera-name validation
Upgrade spec 06 import apply: create cameras from config, route polygons to per-camera tabs, merge-by-name
QA: cross-camera leakage matrix (canvas/list/YAML on every switch), 1440p viewport lock, 900px stack, migration from v2 data, camera-delete undo atomicity, 20-camera tab strip

docs_md:
## Multi-Camera Workspace
A camera tab strip above the canvas gives every camera its own frame, polygons, and YAML. The YAML panel gains a scope toggle: 'This camera' emits a paste-ready polygon snippet; 'All cameras' emits the complete cameras: config. Camera Builder feeds the workspace; YAML import populates it. Pre-existing single-camera work lands on a 'default' tab and can be assigned to a real camera.

stub_suggestions.purpose: Tab strip renders purely from state

metrics: 0 cross-camera leakage defects in the QA switch matrix (canvas, item list, YAML checked after every switch permutation)
Full-config export for a 3-camera workspace passes Frigate config validation unmodified
Viewport lock preserved: no scrollbars on app shell at 2560×1440 with tab strip visible and 10 cameras
v2→v3 persisted-data migration succeeds with 100% polygon fidelity in QA fixture

decision_log.rationale: Spec 01 deliberately shaped state as polygonsByCamera so this feature is UI routing plus emission scoping rather than a data-model rewrite. A tab strip above the canvas is the smallest layout change that keeps the canvas the hero per master-spec UI principle #1. Built last because it composes persistence, validation, import, and the toast system.
alternatives_considered: Rejected: dropdown camera selector — hides workspace breadth, more clicks than tabs for the common 2–6 camera case
Rejected: one browser-tab-per-camera with shared storage — cross-tab sync complexity explicitly out of v0.2 scope
Rejected: building multi-camera first — every other spec would rebase onto the schema change; sequencing it last makes it additive
tradeoffs: Tab strip consumes ~36px of vertical canvas space
In-memory images are lost on refresh by design (re-upload prompt per camera)
schemaVersion bump means pre-08 stored data needs the migration path tested
decision_log.confidence_rationale: State model is pre-fitted, but this spec integrates four prior specs and carries the main layout-regression risk — hence 8 points and last position.

defaults_applied: Assuming 'This camera' snippet scope omits ffmpeg/detect (oq_001 recommended)
Assuming the Camera Builder remains a separate tab in v0.2 (not folded into the editor) to limit layout churn
Assuming horizontal scroll, not overflow dropdown, for many cameras
Assuming camera identity is the generated id, with name as a mutable label
